disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison's disease) maintained on twice-daily oral hydrocortisone
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
What medications does he normally take?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has Addison's disease — his body can't make the stress hormone on its own, so he takes steroid tablets morning and night.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Missed both oral steroid doses because he could not keep tablets down after vomiting from a viral gastrointestinal illness
trigger_question_examples: Has he been able to take his usual tablets?
Has he kept his steroid doses down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — he threw up twice yesterday and hasn't managed his morning or evening steroid tablets since.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound weakness with postural dizziness/pre-syncope and inability to stand — proxy for circulatory compromise
trigger_question_examples: How is he when he tries to sit or stand?
Is he feeling faint or dizzy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He goes really dizzy and feels like he'll pass out the moment he tries to sit up, and he can't stand at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive drowsiness and confusion over the last two hours (altered mental status)
trigger_question_examples: Is he alert and making sense?
Has his level of alertness changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Over the last couple of hours he's gone very drowsy and muddled — he keeps drifting off and isn't really making sense.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Preceding mild viral gastroenteritis with two episodes of vomiting and anorexia the day before
trigger_question_examples: Has he had any vomiting or diarrhoea?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started with what seemed like a stomach bug yesterday — he vomited twice and didn't want to eat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Generalised abdominal pain without localising features
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any tummy pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He says his whole tummy aches, but he can't point to one spot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels profoundly dehydrated despite oral fluid intake; reduced urine output
trigger_question_examples: Has he been able to drink and pass urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's been sipping water but still feels parched, his mouth is dry and he's hardly passed any urine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Household may hold an emergency hydrocortisone injection kit for use when unwell
trigger_question_examples: Do you have an emergency hydrocortisone injection at home?
Were you given an injection kit for when he's too unwell to take tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I think the specialist gave us an emergency injection kit for times like this, but I've never had to use it and I'm not sure I'd know how.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm frightened of giving an injection — are you sure I should?
